transcript:
Three hundred faint rings and rounded squares are layered on top of each other, breathing in and out of visibility — what you're watching is really just circles quietly growing, shrinking, and rotating, but the overlap makes it read as a dense, ever-shifting wire sculpture.

Here's the trick: none of this is one system talking to itself. In the style block, each shape gets assigned a class like freq-2 or freq-23, and each of those runs its own keyframe animation — resonance-2, resonance-3, all the way to resonance-23 — lasting exactly that many seconds. Down in the script, the comment literally says "prime number periods ensure the system will eventually synchronize in beautiful, rare moments before dissolving back into chaotic interference patterns." Because 2, 3, 5, 7, 11... share no common factors, the shapes drift in and out of phase with each other almost forever before any two cycles line up again.

So that flickering you see isn't randomness — it's arithmetic. You're watching prime numbers argue with each other, and every so often, briefly, they agree.
